free_text: Alpha induced reactions on natSn: An experimental study
of excitation functions and possible production
pathways
14.02 microns natural Sn target foils
12.0 microns natural Ti monitor foils from Goodfellow
Standard stacked foil irradiation method was used, Ti
foils served as energy degraders and beam monitors.
The irradiation was done at the VUB cyclotron with
70 nA beam intensity for 3960 seconds in a Faraday-type
target holder. The incident energy was determined by
using the monitor reaction.
Gamma spectra were measured non-destructively on a
HPGe detector.
Decay data were taken from the NUDAT.
Samples were measured several times started shortly
after EOB and followed the decay for 4 weeks.
Uncertainties of the beams were estimated by
combining the uncertainties of the primary beam energy
0+-0.1MeV, the energy degradation and the energy
straggling which was 2.7 MeV in the last foil.
Uncertainties of the cross sections were
estimated in the knowledge of relative uncertainties:
number of the bombarding particles (5%), number of the
target nuclei (5%), decay data (1-5%), detector
efficiency (5%), peak area (1-5%).
Error in the number of bombarding
particles (5%),
Error in the number of target nuclei (5%),
Error in detector efficiency (5%)
The measured experimental data were compared
with earlier literature values.
The measured experimental data were compared to
and analyzed with theoretical calculations based on
the model code ALICE-IPPE.
R.A. Rebeles, 2007.
